phases.name: MtlsResolve port + sim adapter + ServiceBackendsResolve host adapter (the #178 anti-corruption boundary)

steps.name: Define the MtlsResolve driven port: 3-variant MtlsResolution + 2-field ResolvedBackend (C1 + C2)
criteria: New file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mtls_resolve.rs declares `pub trait MtlsResolve` with exactly one resolve method whose signature is `async fn resolve(&self, orig_dst: SocketAddrV4) -> Result<MtlsResolution, MtlsResolveError>` plus a `probe` method (Earned-Trust, principle 12) — no other public methods on the trait.

C1: `pub enum MtlsResolution { Mesh(ResolvedBackend), NonMesh, MeshUnreachable }` — EXACTLY these three variants, names fixed. The per-arm enforce/pass-through/fail-closed rustdoc semantics from ADR-0071 § 'The new driven port' (C1) and feature-delta § 'MtlsResolve port contract' are reproduced VERBATIM on the variant rustdoc: Mesh->ENFORCE (only handshake arm), NonMesh->PASS-THROUGH (cleartext by design, classification arm, not an error), MeshUnreachable->FAIL-CLOSED (no cleartext, the footgun the enrollment model removes).

C2: `pub struct ResolvedBackend { pub addr: SocketAddrV4, pub expected_svid: Option<SpiffeId> }` — bounded to EXACTLY two fields, no third. Rustdoc states v1 adapters return `expected_svid: None` (the join is #178; filling it is boundary divergence).
`MtlsResolveError` is a thiserror enum with a distinct variant per failure mode (store-unreadable at resolve time, etc.) per development.md § Errors; no `Internal(String)` catch-all.
Trait + types re-exported from crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mod.rs alongside the existing `pub use mtls_enforcement::{...}` block; `pub mod mtls_resolve;` added in module-list order.

Default-lane proptest: a roundtrip/exhaustiveness proptest target `mtls_resolution_classification_is_total` asserting every (addr-present, addr-absent, store-fail) classification input maps to exactly one of the three arms (no panic, no fourth state). trybuild not required here (no compile-time substitutability invariant).
dst-lint clean: the port adds no I/O to a core-class crate (trait + `#[async_trait]` only, mirroring mtls_enforcement).
steps.implementation_notes: Implements C1 (3-variant MtlsResolution sum type, NOT Option) and C2 (2-field ResolvedBackend) PINNED in ADR-0071 § 'The new driven port' and feature-delta § 'MtlsResolve port contract'. SpiffeId is at overdrive-core/src/id.rs:240 (re-exported from crate root). Model on the existing mtls_enforcement.rs trait shape (pure trait + #[async_trait], off the core I/O surface; dst-lint scans core-class crates). CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design — never invent API surface': do NOT add a fourth variant, a third ResolvedBackend field, or a second resolve method. CLAUDE.md § 'Trait definitions specify behavior, not just signature': the per-arm rustdoc IS the contract the DST equivalence test (05-02) exercises — write it verbatim from the ADR. Test tier: default-lane proptest (pure classification). No external integration (intra-cluster observation-store boundary).

steps.name: SimMtlsResolve scriptable sim adapter (overdrive-sim) + DST controllability
criteria: New `SimMtlsResolve` in overdrive-sim implements `MtlsResolve`; constructor takes a scripted `BTreeMap<SocketAddrV4, MtlsResolution>` (or equivalent deterministic table) so a DST test can pin each orig_dst to a chosen arm.

`resolve(orig_dst)` returns the scripted arm for a known addr and a configurable default (`NonMesh`) for an unknown addr; a separate scripted-fault mode returns `MeshUnreachable` / a typed store-fail error to exercise the fail-closed and probe-refusal paths.
`probe()` is scriptable to succeed or to return `health.startup.refused`-shaped failure so 04-* and 05-* can exercise wire-then-probe-then-use.
Iteration order is `BTreeMap`-deterministic per development.md § Ordered-collection choice (the table is iterated/observed under DST).
Default-lane unit tests cover: known-addr->scripted arm, unknown-addr->NonMesh default, fault-mode->MeshUnreachable, probe success + probe refusal.
Declared in overdrive-sim under the `adapter-sim` class; `integration-tests = []` feature present per workspace convention.
steps.implementation_notes: Driven ports table (feature-delta): SimMtlsResolve lives in overdrive-sim (adapter-sim). Model on the existing SimObservationStore adapter (crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs:215 — BTreeMap rationale precedent). This is the DST-controllable counterpart that keeps the per-connection resolve injectable so the worker (04-02) and the walking skeleton (05-*) can pin classification deterministically. development.md § 'Production code is not shaped by simulation': production must not carry logic to make the sim behave; the sim must model the real classification faithfully. Test tier: default-lane unit.

steps.name: ServiceBackendsResolve host adapter (reads service_backends ∩ running; expected_svid: None) + Earned-Trust probe
criteria: New `ServiceBackendsResolve` host adapter implements `MtlsResolve` by building an in-RAM, ownership-aware `addr -> {service -> Backend}` reverse index of the `running` `service_backends` set (F-A, ratified 2026-06-17 — keyed per contributing service at an addr, NOT a flat `addr -> Backend` with global last-writer-wins; a service's backend-set shrink evicts only THAT service's contribution; classification is any-healthy-at-addr, deterministic) via List-then-Watch over the `ObservationStore` (C4/D-TME-11 REVISED 2026-06-17; F4/relist-trigger REFINED 2026-06-17): List-at-probe via the keyless enumerate `ObservationStore::all_service_backends_rows` (SHIPPED 25e7acf3), Watch via the NEW lag-surfacing `ObservationStore::subscribe_all_events` (NOT the lossy `subscribe_all` — its item type `ObservationSubscription` carries no loss signal and both adapters strip `RecvError::Lagged` internally), relist on `SubscriptionEvent::Lagged { missed }`; `resolve(orig_dst)` is a point-lookup into the index, NOT the per-`ServiceId` `service_backends_rows` point query (the adapter holds no `ServiceId`). An orig_dst that matches a `running` mesh backend in the index -> `Mesh(ResolvedBackend { addr, expected_svid: None })`.

C2 ENFORCED: `expected_svid` is `None` for EVERY backend — the adapter does NOT thread `IdentityRead` and does NOT join identity facts (the join is #178; doing it here is a boundary-divergence rejection per CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design').

Classification is in the adapter, never inferred by the worker, and MUST match the shipped 01-01 port error split (crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/mtls_resolve.rs) — do NOT collapse a store-LAYER fault into `MeshUnreachable`: orig_dst with no `running` mesh backend -> `NonMesh`; a matched mesh backend present-but-unreachable / its required identity facts absent -> `MeshUnreachable` (a per-connection classification); a store-LAYER read fault at resolve time (poisoned handle, corrupt table, errored subscription) that is NOT a per-connection classification -> `Err(MtlsResolveError::StoreUnreadable)`, NOT `MeshUnreachable`. Each path has a distinct typed error/branch (development.md § 'Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants').

Earned-Trust `probe()` = the List-at-probe gate: it List-loads the current `service_backends` snapshot via `all_service_backends_rows` into the in-RAM index BEFORE the gate opens / before any `resolve` is served (so the index is never empty-but-trusted — closes #237 cold-start, mirrors Cilium's ListDone-gates-synced); on an unreadable store it returns a structured `health.startup.refused` event and refuses — it NEVER silently returns empty/`NonMesh` (silent-empty = the silent-cleartext footgun). Probe spec carries an explicit fault-injection scenario (store-read error) it must survive by refusing.

Default-lane DST/unit tests against a tiny `SimObservationStore` dataset: running-backend-hit->Mesh{addr,None}; no-mesh-backend->NonMesh; matched-backend-present-but-unreachable->MeshUnreachable; store-LAYER-read-fault->Err(StoreUnreadable) (NOT MeshUnreachable); cold-start List-at-probe seeds the index before the gate opens (a pre-existing service_backends row is resolvable on the FIRST resolve, no empty-but-trusted window); a `SubscriptionEvent::Lagged { missed }` delivered by `subscribe_all_events` triggers a re-List (the index recovers the dropped row, not silently stale — completeness: a dropped update is delivered OR signaled-then-relisted, never silently lost); probe-on-unreadable-store->refuses (health.startup.refused), probe-on-readable-store->ok.

Mutation kill-rate >= 80% on the classification + index-eviction logic. The real mutation surface (there is NO `>`/`>=` running filter in the adapter): the `BackendIndex::classify` three match arms + the `backend.healthy` guard (a dropped arm or flipped guard = silent cleartext to a should-be-mesh peer, OR a should-be-`NonMesh` egress forced to fail-closed), and `apply_row` (the per-service eviction loop + the V4-row filter that decides which contributing service's backends are added/evicted at an addr).
Adapter home justified in a module-level rustdoc (see implementation_notes).
steps.implementation_notes: ADAPTER HOME DECISION: overdrive-control-plane (adapter-host). Rationale: (a) the ObservationStore read surfaces are consumed adapter-host-side; overdrive-control-plane already wires ObservationStore-backed adapters (veth_provisioner, action_shim) and is the boot composition site where the resolve probe must refuse. (b) overdrive-dataplane is the kTLS/map executor (wrong concern — map writes, not a resolve query). (c) a NEW crate is rejected per Reuse Analysis (prefer EXTEND an existing adapter-host crate). Implements Q3/D-TME-6 + the C1 classification mapping + the Earned-Trust probe from ADR-0071 § Enforcement. C4/D-TME-11 READ MECHANISM (REVISED 2026-06-17, resolve-index-coherence research; F4/relist-trigger REFINED 2026-06-17, ratified option 2): List-then-Watch + relist-on-`Lagged`, REVERSING the prior observe-only / 'no new trait method' constraint. THIS STEP IS A FOLLOW-UP on commit 25e7acf3, which already shipped: the keyless List enumerate (#237 closed), List-at-probe, the single-owner background drain (F2 dissolved structurally), watch-close->Err(StoreUnreadable), and the relist() machinery (exercised at probe and on watch-close). The ONLY missing leg this step adds is the `Lagged` relist TRIGGER. (1) List enumerate `async fn all_service_backends_rows(&self) -> Result<Vec<ServiceBackendRow>, ObservationStoreError>;` (observation_store.rs:1440) is SHIPPED — confirm + reuse, do not re-add. (2) List-at-probe SHIPPED — confirm + reuse. (3) Watch: consume the NEW lag-surfacing subscription, NOT the lossy `subscribe_all`. RATIONALE this is a NEW surface: `subscribe_all` returns `ObservationSubscription = Box<dyn Stream<Item = ObservationRow>>` (observation_store.rs:1149) which carries NO loss signal, and BOTH store adapters strip `RecvError::Lagged` INSIDE `subscribe_all` before any consumer sees it (overdrive-sim `ok_or_skip`; overdrive-store-local `filter_map(Result::ok)` observation_backend.rs:506 / `filter_map`-on-`Lagged` redb_backend.rs:368-373). PIN the lag-surfacing surface on the ObservationStore trait (observation_store.rs): `pub enum SubscriptionEvent { Row(ObservationRow), Lagged { missed: u64 } }` (a DOMAIN event — the adapter maps `broadcast::RecvError::Lagged(n)` to `Lagged { missed: n }` at the adapter boundary; the core trait NEVER names a tokio type), `pub type LagAwareSubscription = Box<dyn Stream<Item = SubscriptionEvent> + Send + Unpin>;`, and `async fn subscribe_all_events(&self) -> Result<LagAwareSubscription, ObservationStoreError>;`. Implement `subscribe_all_events` on BOTH store adapters (overdrive-store-local/src/observation_backend.rs map the broadcast stream's `Lagged(n)` arm to `SubscriptionEvent::Lagged { missed: n }` instead of `filter_map`-dropping it; overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs map its `ok_or_skip` equivalent likewise) + any in-crate test doubles. NOTE (F-B, reconciled 2026-06-17 — supersedes the original 'KEEP `subscribe_all` AS-IS for ~20 consumers' instruction): the lossy `subscribe_all` + `ObservationSubscription` alias were already DELETED single-cut in commit 36a79762 and ALL consumers migrated to `subscribe_all_events` — so `subscribe_all_events` is the SOLE observation-subscription surface in tree; confirm this state, do NOT re-add `subscribe_all` and do NOT keep a parallel lossy path (that earlier 'dedicated method bounds blast radius' framing was overtaken by the single-cut per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`/`feedback_delete_dont_gate`). (4) relist-on-`Lagged` (the TRIGGER, this step's core add): the single-owner drain consumes `subscribe_all_events()`; on `SubscriptionEvent::Row(row)` apply the row to the index; on `SubscriptionEvent::Lagged { missed }` re-List via the SHIPPED `all_service_backends_rows()` and rebuild/merge the index using the SHIPPED relist() machinery — MUST NOT silently discard the loss (closes F4 with a completeness guarantee). (5) Concurrency: the single-owner drain is SHIPPED — wire the new trigger into it, do not re-architect. The in-RAM index + drain shape is already chosen (25e7acf3); the CONTRACT (List-at-probe + Watch-via-subscribe_all_events + relist-on-`Lagged` + single-owner) is pinned. F-A INDEX SHAPE (ratified 2026-06-17 — option b): the index is ownership-aware — keyed `addr -> {service -> Backend}` (each contributing service's backend at an addr tracked separately), NOT a flat `addr -> Backend` with global last-writer-wins eviction. `apply_row`/eviction: a service's backend-set shrink evicts only THAT service's contribution at the addr; an addr stays resolvable as long as ANY service still claims a healthy backend there; `classify` is any-healthy-at-addr (deterministic — orig_dst hits `Mesh` iff some service still claims a `running`/healthy backend at that addr — NOT last-writer-wins). This removes the unstated 'one (IP:port) belongs to at most one service' cross-component invariant a flat index would rely on; v1 single-node is structurally addr-exclusive (per-addr service set size-1 today) so the shape is defensive against multi-node/future writers, NOT a behaviour change. The index structure is adapter-internal — the public MtlsResolve contract + the NonMesh/MeshUnreachable/Err arms are UNCHANGED. F-B (reconciled 2026-06-17): `subscribe_all_events` is the SOLE observation-subscription surface — the lossy `subscribe_all` + `ObservationSubscription` alias were DELETED single-cut in commit 36a79762 and every consumer migrated; do NOT re-add `subscribe_all` or keep a parallel lossy path (that earlier 'dedicated method bounds blast radius / ~20 consumers stay untouched' framing was superseded by the single-cut). PUBLIC MtlsResolve API unchanged (growth confined to the ObservationStore driven port). ServiceBackendRowV1 fields: service_id, vip, backends: Vec<Backend>, updated_at (observation_store.rs:945). The `running` filter is the adapter's responsibility — confirm the running-intersection source (alloc_status running set) during implementation; if the precise `running` join surface is ambiguous, STOP and surface the gap (do not invent a running-set reader). C2 shell discipline: NO IdentityRead, expected_svid always None. Test tier: default-lane DST/unit + mutation.

phases.name: Per-workload netns+veth provisioner (extend veth_provisioner) + converge-on-boot host prereqs + resolv.conf injection

steps.name: Per-alloc netns+veth derivation + converge_steps (pure, default-lane) — parameterize veth_provisioner per-allocation
criteria: Introduce a `NetSlot(u16)` newtype (bound `0..=NET_SLOT_MAX`, NET_SLOT_MAX=4095) with FromStr/Display/serde/validating `new` + `to_hex4()` (per development.md § Newtype completeness). The 4-char hex ceiling is DERIVED from IFNAMSIZ (assert `WORKLOAD_HOST_VETH_PREFIX.len() + 4 <= 15` in a unit/doctest), NOT a magic number (D-TME-12).

EXTEND veth_provisioner.rs: a pure `derive_workload_netns_plan(slot: NetSlot, responder_addr) -> WorkloadNetnsPlan` computing the SLOT-DERIVED per-alloc netns name (`ovd-ns-<4hex-slot>`, 11 chars <= NAME_MAX(255) AND <= IFNAMSIZ, bounded by construction — B3: slot-keyed like the veths, NOT `ovd-ns-<alloc>` which overflows NAME_MAX at 260 chars for a 253-char alloc id, the same pigeonhole/ceiling class as B1), the SLOT-DERIVED veth pair names (`ovd-wl-<4hex-slot>` in-netns end, `ovd-hv-<4hex-slot>` host-side end — 11 chars each), the slot-derived /30 subnet (carved from a fixed per-host `WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE` /16: subnet = base + slot*4 /30; the 4096-slot space tiles a /18 within the /16, NOT the whole /16 — N2), in-netns + host-side addresses (host=net+1, workload=net+2; a /30 ALWAYS has two usable hosts so no Option/network() fallback — S2), default route (gateway=host_addr), and the host routing prereqs. The `alloc_id` parameter is DROPPED (B3): with the netns name slot-keyed, the alloc id derives nothing (all three names + subnet are slot-keyed; responder_addr is passthrough), so carrying it would be a speculative unused param — the alloc<->slot binding's home is the 02-04 allocator map. Pure derivation, default lane, compiles on every platform; never persisted (development.md § 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). The subnet is NO LONGER a caller parameter — the derivation owns slot->/30 (S1).

IFNAMSIZ + NAME_MAX + collision-freedom proptest invariants over the FULL 0..=NET_SLOT_MAX slot space: (a) `host_veth.len() <= 15 && workload_veth.len() <= 15 && netns.len() <= 15` for every slot (all three names slot-keyed, so the netns name is bounded by the SAME 11-char ceiling as the veths — the symmetric companion to the veth IFNAMSIZ invariant the first cut had for the veths only; B3); (b) distinct slots => distinct names (netns + both veths) AND distinct /30 subnets (collision-free by construction, NOT by hash — D-TME-12 / B1 / B3). A previous golden assertion `ovd-hv-payments-0` (17 chars, IFNAMSIZ-overflow) is REMOVED; the prior netns test capping the alloc id at 21 chars is REMOVED (the netns name no longer depends on the alloc id, so there is no alloc-id-length axis to vary).

EXTEND `workload_converge_steps` (the per-alloc converger): maps an `ObservedWorkloadVeth` snapshot of actual netns/veth kernel state to the minimal ordered step set converging to the desired COMPLETE (carry-a-packet) shape — create netns, create veth pair, move in-netns end into the netns, address both ends, host-side end up, IN-NETNS end up (`SetWorkloadVethUp`) AND netns loopback up (`SetLoopbackUp`) — both ordered AFTER MoveWorkloadEndIntoNetns (B2; a veth forwards only when both ends are up, and a fresh netns has `lo` down), default route in-netns, AND the spike-proven host prereqs as steps: `ip_forward=1`, `rp_filter` relaxation SPLIT into global (`all`+`lo`, `RelaxGlobalRpFilter`) vs per-host-veth (`RelaxHostVethRpFilter`, strict by default on a fresh veth — S3), and `tx off` (csum invariant, bpf.md Rule 2) on both ends. `ObservedWorkloadVeth` carries the matching facts: `workload_veth_up`, `lo_up`, `rp_filter_global_relaxed`, `host_veth_rp_filter_relaxed` (the last two replace the single lossy `rp_filter_relaxed` bool).

Idempotent converge-on-boot semantics preserved per ADR-0061 / reconcilers.md Bar-1: complete pair -> all-noop; half-provisioned -> completed in place; corrupted (netns present, veth absent) -> recreated; never tears down a usable pair.

Pure-function default-lane proptest/unit: `workload_netns_converge_steps_are_minimal_and_idempotent` — observed==desired yields empty step set; each partial observation (including the new `workload_veth_up` / `lo_up` / split-rp_filter facts) yields exactly the missing steps; re-applying the step set is a no-op.
Mutation kill-rate >= 80% on `NetSlot` + `derive_workload_netns_plan` + `workload_converge_steps` (the converge diff is the canonical reconciler-logic mutation target).
No Tier-3 in THIS step (pure derivation only); the real `ip netns`/`ip link` execution is 02-02. The stateful slot allocator is 02-04, NOT this step (this step takes `slot` as a pure input).
steps.implementation_notes: Q2/D-TME-2 + D-TME-12 (re-implementation after the 02-01 review NEEDS_REVISION — deliver/reviews/02-01.md; pinned contract in design/wave-decisions.md § 'D-TME-12 — pinned API contract'). EXTEND veth_provisioner (proven pure-derive + converge-on-boot shape), parameterized per-alloc by `NetSlot`. The netns name + both veth NAMES + the /30 subnet ALL derive from the slot (collision-free by construction; a hash is WRONG by pigeonhole — see D-TME-12; B3 slot-keyed the netns name too). Build ONLY the surface D-TME-12 names: `NetSlot` newtype + `NET_SLOT_MAX` + `WORKLOAD_SUBNET_BASE`, the exact `derive_workload_netns_plan(slot, responder_addr)` signature (alloc_id DROPPED — B3), the listed `WorkloadVethStep` variants (incl. `SetWorkloadVethUp`/`SetLoopbackUp`, split `RelaxGlobalRpFilter`/`RelaxHostVethRpFilter`), the `ObservedWorkloadVeth` facts (incl. `workload_veth_up`/`lo_up`/`rp_filter_global_relaxed`/`host_veth_rp_filter_relaxed`) — do NOT invent additional API (CLAUDE.md § 'Implement to the design'). `SetLoopbackUp` emit-condition is `!netns_present || !lo_up` (NOT `pair_rebuilt` — `lo` is netns-scoped, survives a veth-only rebuild; S4-corrected SSOT). `let _ = plan;` in the pure diff is INTENTIONAL (N1 confirmed). Mirror the existing derive_veth_plan / converge_steps / VethStep / ObservedVeth shapes (veth_provisioner.rs:71+). The host prereqs are from spike findings-egress-tproxy.md § 'Design implications' point 4 + § 'Edge cases' (leg-dial SO_MARK belongs to the agent dial in 03-03, NOT the provisioner). reconcilers.md Bar-1: converge, don't apply-once. Per-alloc network reconciler (Bar-2) is the deferred #197/#234 promotion — do NOT build a full Reconciler trait impl here. The stateful slot allocator is 02-04. Test tier: default-lane proptest + mutation (pure logic only).

steps.name: Real netns+veth provision execution (Tier-3 Lima) — idempotent ip netns/ip link converge against a real kernel
criteria: EXTEND `provision` (or a per-alloc `provision_workload_netns`) — the real `ip(8)` / `ip netns` shell-out, `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` production: OBSERVE actual netns/veth kernel state, compute the 02-01 converge steps, EXECUTE each idempotently (swallow EEXIST / 'File exists' on netns/link/addr/route add), refuse boot on a non-benign failure (the veth_provisioner converge-on-boot precedent — that IS its probe).

Tier-3 real-kernel acceptance (gated `integration-tests`, run via `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`): provisioning a fresh alloc creates the netns + veth pair with the in-netns end inside the netns, host-side end up, IN-NETNS end up, netns `lo` up (B2 — assert all three up-states; a veth forwards only when both ends are up and a fresh netns has `lo` down), addresses + default route present; re-running provision is all-noop (idempotent); a half-provisioned netns (veth absent) is completed; teardown removes the netns + veth leaving zero residue (no netns, no link, no leftover route) per spike teardown evidence.

Tier-3 assertions are on observable kernel side effects (`ip netns list`, `ip -n <ns> link` for up-state of BOTH ends + `lo`, `ip -n <ns> addr`, `ip -n <ns> route`, `ethtool -k` for tx off) — never on internal reachability (testing.md Tier-3 assertion rules).

Spike-proven host prereqs verified present after provision: `ip_forward=1`, `rp_filter` relaxed BOTH globally (`all`+`lo`) AND on the per-host-veth knob (S3 — the two are separate facts/steps), `tx off` on both ends.

Test lives at crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/workload_netns_provision.rs, wired through tests/integration.rs; uses tempdir/per-alloc names to avoid cross-test collision (testing.md leaked-resource discipline); RAII teardown.
NO `--no-run` gate — the fixtures must actually RUN under Lima (testing.md: a --no-run gate is green even when every fixture refuses at boot).
steps.implementation_notes: Tier-3 MANDATORY: no Tier-2 backstop for netns/veth (spike.md + testing.md). Run under Lima as root (cargo xtask lima run --). Mirror the increment-b spike recipe (spike-scratch/increment-b/ — the PROVEN Rust + ip/nft egress harness) for the ip netns / veth / ip_forward / rp_filter recipe; do NOT copy it into crates/ (copy the recipe, write fresh production code). Reuse overdrive-testing fixtures (NetNs/ThreeIfaceTopology) where they fit (dev-dep, adapter-host). reconcilers.md: 'an error whose remediation names a manual fix on an appliance OS with no operator shell' is a symptom — the system must self-heal via converge-on-boot. Test tier: Tier-3 real-kernel under Lima.

steps.name: resolv.conf injection into the per-workload netns (Q5a name-layer integration) — one converge step on the provisioner
criteria: EXTEND the provisioner with an idempotent converge step that writes the node-local DNS responder address into the per-workload netns's own `/etc/resolv.conf` (per-netns mount, the stock `ip netns` convention) — the Fly.io `fdaa::3` injection model.

The responder address is an INPUT to the provisioner (derived/configured), not hardcoded; the injection is part of the same converge-on-boot pass, so a netns whose resolv.conf cannot be written refuses on the same boot path as the netns/veth provisioning (ADR-0071 § Enforcement: 'a netns whose resolv.conf cannot be written refuses on the same boot path').
SCOPE GUARDRAIL: this step wires ONLY the injection. It does NOT build the #61 DNS responder daemon and does NOT build the #167 VIP allocator (named dependencies; building either is scope creep).
Tier-3 acceptance: after provision, `ip netns exec <ns> cat /etc/resolv.conf` shows the injected responder address; re-running is a no-op; the file is per-netns (host /etc/resolv.conf unaffected).
Default-lane unit covers the pure 'what should resolv.conf contain' derivation; the real per-netns write is the Tier-3 assertion.
Test added to crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/workload_netns_provision.rs (same scenario file as 02-02).
steps.implementation_notes: Q5a / D-TME-9 + ADR-0071 fact 8. resolv.conf injection is an EXTEND of the netns provisioner (zero new CREATE-NEW per Reuse Analysis). The returned DNS shape is HEADLESS v1 (D-TME-10): the responder returns a running service_backends addr — but that responder daemon is #61 and is NOT built here. The single-source invariant (DNS returns a service_backends addr; MtlsResolve reads a service_backends addr) is exercised in 05-02, not here. Test tier: default-lane unit (derivation) + Tier-3 (real per-netns write).

steps.name: Per-host NetSlot allocator + C3 on_alloc_running/on_alloc_terminal lifecycle wiring (assign-smallest-free / release; provision call site)
criteria: CREATE-NEW a per-host `NetSlot` allocator (D-TME-12): a free-list assigning the smallest-free `NetSlot` (0..=NET_SLOT_MAX) on alloc start and releasing it on alloc terminal. Pure assign/release decision logic separated from the lifecycle wiring so the assign-smallest-free / release / double-release / exhaustion behaviour is default-lane unit-testable; the held slot↔alloc map is the allocator's state. Single-node trivial — NOT distributed IPAM, NOT the #167 VIP allocator (do NOT pull #167 in).

Slot claim is atomic (development.md § 'Check-and-act must be atomic'): assigning the smallest-free slot and inserting it into the held map is one operation — no contains-then-insert TOCTOU. Re-assigning an already-held alloc returns its existing slot (idempotent re-entry of on_alloc_running); releasing an unheld alloc is a no-op (idempotent teardown).

Wire the allocator at the action-shim C3 lifecycle hooks: `on_alloc_running` assigns the slot, records the alloc<->slot binding in the held map (the alloc id's home — the netns/veth/subnet are slot-keyed, NOT alloc-keyed, B3), then calls the 02-02 `provision_workload_netns` with `derive_workload_netns_plan(slot, responder_addr)` — BEFORE MtlsInterceptWorker::start_alloc and BEFORE Driver::start (the netns+veth must exist before the workload is spawned into it; C3 / ADR-0071 fact 1). `on_alloc_terminal` tears down the netns+veth then releases the slot.

Exhaustion (no free slot <= NET_SLOT_MAX) is a typed error that refuses the alloc (structured event), NOT a panic and NOT a silent reuse of a held slot (a reused slot would collide two live allocs onto one veth/subnet — the exact B1 collision the slot model exists to prevent).

Default-lane unit + mutation >= 80% on the pure assign/release decision logic. The C3 wiring's real provision call is covered by the 02-02 Tier-3 acceptance (this step adds the allocator + the call site; it does not re-prove the ip-netns execution).

Persist-inputs discipline (development.md): the slot↔alloc map is in-RAM ephemeral runtime state rebuilt on restart by re-assigning for every still-Running alloc (mirrors the IdentityMgr held-snapshot shape) — single-node Phase 1, no cross-restart slot persistence needed; if a later phase needs slot stability across restart that is a tracked follow-up, NOT this step.
steps.implementation_notes: D-TME-12 (see design/wave-decisions.md § 'D-TME-12 — pinned API contract' → 'Slot-allocator home'). The allocator is the STATEFUL companion to 02-01's PURE slot-keyed derivation: 02-01 takes `slot` as an input, this step is what hands out the slot. Home = the C3 `on_alloc_running` hook (assign) / `on_alloc_terminal` (release) — the SAME hook that owns netns creation (action_shim/mod.rs:1002/1152 fire on_alloc_running; :851/1227 fire on_alloc_terminal). Mirror the IdentityMgr `Arc<RwLock<BTreeMap<...>>>` held-state shape (in-RAM, rebuilt-on-restart). Use a `ClaimSet`-style atomic claim (overdrive-core) for the slot map if it fits, else branch on the insert return — no contains-then-insert. Build ONLY the allocator + the C3 call site; do NOT add a full Reconciler trait impl (the per-alloc network reconciler Bar-2 promotion is deferred #197/#234). Test tier: default-lane unit + mutation (pure assign/release); the provision call is Tier-3-covered by 02-02.

phases.name: Egress nft-TPROXY install + accept_outbound getsockname orig-dst + F5 leg-dial SO_MARK (Tier-3 egress capture)

steps.name: install_outbound_tproxy — egress nft-TPROXY on the host-side veth, sibling of install_inbound_tproxy (shared routing infra)
criteria: EXTEND mtls_intercept.rs with `install_outbound_tproxy(host_veth, agent_leg_f_port) -> Result<TproxyInterceptGuard>` — an nft PREROUTING rule on the per-workload host-side veth matching the workload's egress (ingressing the host veth) and redirecting to the agent's leg-F `IP_TRANSPARENT` listener.

REUSE verbatim: `ensure_shared_routing_infra` (the shared NFT_TABLE `overdrive-mtls` + `prerouting` NFT_CHAIN + TPROXY_FWMARK 0x1 + TPROXY_RT_TABLE 100 + the F5 `MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK` head-exemption), the by-handle RAII `TproxyInterceptGuard` Drop (removes only its one rule), the idempotent ensure, and the canonical zero-padded-mark dump-parse dedup logic. The egress rule is one MORE per-virt rule appended to the SAME shared chain (could share the inbound chain).

Default-lane unit: dump-parse tests for the egress rule shape (mirror the existing inbound dump-parse unit tests); idempotent re-install appends no duplicate; guard Drop removes exactly its rule by handle.
PREROUTING `priority mangle` is the hook (same as inbound) — NO OUTPUT chain, NO separate egress hook (spike findings § 'priority mangle PREROUTING is the correct hook').
The real egress capture (does it fire for netns egress at the veth ingress) is the Tier-3 03-03 assertion; this step proves the install/teardown/dedup shape default-lane.
Mutation >= 80% on any pure rule-construction / dedup-parse helper added.
steps.implementation_notes: EXTEND base: mtls_intercept.rs (install_inbound_tproxy at :239, ensure_shared_routing_infra at :293, NFT_TABLE/NFT_CHAIN/TPROXY_FWMARK/TPROXY_RT_TABLE consts :56-74, TproxyInterceptGuard :504, MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK via overdrive_core::dataplane). Spike findings-egress-tproxy.md: the egress rule is a LITERAL mirror of install_inbound_tproxy applied to egress — PREROUTING nft-TPROXY on the host-side veth -> fwmark -> ip rule -> local route table -> IP_TRANSPARENT leg-F -> getsockname. Reuse over novelty (Reuse Analysis: EXTEND). Test tier: default-lane unit (dump-parse) here; Tier-3 fire-test in 03-03.

steps.name: accept_outbound_leg recovers orig-dst via getsockname (symmetric with inbound); build Routed::Outbound { peer }
criteria: EXTEND `accept_outbound_leg` to recover the real peer via `getsockname_orig` on the TPROXY-intercepted leg-F socket (the exact `getsockname` mechanism already proven for inbound `accept_inbound_leg`), building `Routed::Outbound { peer }` from the recovered orig-dst — NOT from a declared-peer slot.

The `MtlsEnforcement` 4-method port is UNTOUCHED: `enforce` still takes `Routed::Outbound { peer }`; only how the worker obtains `peer` changes (getsockname). NO new method, NO new variant on the port (frozen).
The accepted-socket peer is the workload's veth address; `getsockname` returns the dialed orig-dst (NOT leg-F) — the correctness oracle per spike evidence.
Typed-error propagation on getsockname failure via the existing `InterceptError::OrigDst` variant (reused; the OrigDst variant rustdoc already covers accept_outbound_leg per mtls_intercept.rs:113).

Tier-3 in 03-03 proves the real getsockname==dialed-dst recovery on a live connect; this step wires the recovery into the outbound accept path and is unit-covered where the socket plumbing is testable.
No deletion in this step (the declared-peer slot deletion is folded into 04-02 where the resolve consumer orphans it).
steps.implementation_notes: D-TME-4: accept_outbound_leg (mtls_intercept.rs:531) recovers peer via getsockname_orig (:584) — pure reuse of the inbound mechanism (accept_inbound_leg :562 already calls getsockname_orig :572). C-contract: MtlsEnforcement FROZEN — enforce still takes Routed::Outbound { peer }; do NOT add a method/variant. The declared-peer slot (real_peer) is still present at this step; its DELETION is sequenced into 04-02 (the resolve consumer is what orphans it). Test tier: default-lane unit + Tier-3 (03-03).

steps.name: Tier-3 egress capture walking proof: workload connect -> egress nft-TPROXY -> leg-F -> getsockname==dialed-dst + F5 anti-loop SO_MARK
criteria: Tier-3 real-kernel acceptance (Lima, gated `integration-tests`): a workload in its own netns `connect()`s a real backend; the packet egresses the netns via the veth, ingresses the host-side veth, nft-TPROXY at PREROUTING fires and redirects to the agent leg-F IP_TRANSPARENT listener; `getsockname(leg-F-accepted)` == the dialed `(ip,port)` (ADR-0071 Tier-3 obligation (a): egress nft-TPROXY on a per-workload veth recovers orig-dst via getsockname).

F5 (ADR-0071 Tier-3 obligation (b), spike-proven MANDATORY by the negative control): the agent's leg-B/leg-S orig-dst dial carries the leg-dial `SO_MARK` (MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK), so the chain-head `meta mark accept` exemption short-circuits it and the agent's dial reaches the real backend directly (NOT re-captured into leg-F); AND a workload cannot self-exempt (the mark is agent-private, unreachable from the workload netns).

Negative control included: with the F5 exemption removed, the marked dial IS captured (proving the exemption is load-bearing, mirroring the spike's negative control) — OR the test documents the positive+self-exempt-impossible pair if the negative control is too destructive for the shared VM.

with-TPROXY vs without-TPROXY population comparison (debugging.md § 5/§11): without any egress rule the connect reaches the backend directly; with the rule it is redirected to leg-F — isolating 'fired' from 'passed through'.
Assertions on observable kernel/socket side effects only (getsockname return, accepted-socket peer, backend-vs-leg-F delivery), never on program-internal reachability.
Test at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/egress_tproxy_capture.rs (or overdrive-control-plane if the netns provisioner + worker must compose); run via cargo xtask lima run --; NO --no-run.
Records uname -r in the findings/test output (spike.md discipline: verdict pinned to a kernel).
steps.implementation_notes: Tier-3 MANDATORY (no Tier-2 backstop). This is the egress half of the ADR-0071 § Enforcement Tier-3 obligations (a)+(b). The mechanism is PROVEN by increment-b (spike findings-egress-tproxy.md: VERDICT WORKS, getsockname==dialed real-backend, F5 positive+negative controls). Mirror that recipe (real-backend on host lo, workload routes via gateway so egress genuinely ingresses the host veth — not loopback-to-self). The leg-dial SO_MARK is the agent's responsibility (overdrive_core::dataplane::MTLS_LEG_S_DIAL_MARK, identical to inbound, non-optional — proven by the negative control). Depends on 02-02 (real netns) + 03-01 (install) + 03-02 (getsockname recovery). Test tier: Tier-3 real-kernel under Lima; correctness oracle = getsockname orig-dst == dialed dst.

phases.name: Worker rewire: start_alloc swaps to nft-TPROXY (DELETE cgroup surface) + resolve consumer (DELETE declared-peer surface) + action-shim netns C3 wiring

criteria: EXTEND `MtlsInterceptWorker::start_alloc`: install BOTH nft-TPROXY rules (the new outbound `install_outbound_tproxy` on the host-side veth + the existing `install_inbound_tproxy`), stand up the leg-F + leg-C IP_TRANSPARENT listeners, spawn the accept->enforce loops. The cgroup-attach path is removed from the install.

The step that orphans the cgroup path (this swap) IS the step that deletes it — the deletion coincides with the last caller removal (dispatch sequencing rule).

Compile-check clean Lima-routed (cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests); clippy -D warnings clean (no dead_code, no unused_imports left by the deletion).
Tier-3: start_alloc on a real exec alloc installs both rules and both listeners; existing inbound behaviour is preserved (no regression in the inbound capture path).

COMPOSITION-ROOT REWIRE (lib.rs run_server): DELETE the whole `MtlsDataplane::load(&pin_dir)` block (lib.rs:1820-1841 — the load + the MtlsBootError::Load fail-closed arm) AND drop the `dataplane`/`cgroup_root` args from the `MtlsInterceptWorker::new(...)` call (lib.rs:1915-1920 — the worker no longer takes a dataplane or a cgroup root). The SERVICE_MAP fate is RESOLVED — DELETE the whole `MtlsDataplane` struct (see implementation_notes for the file:line evidence): EbpfDataplane pins SERVICE_MAP independently and EARLIER, so nothing in production depends on MtlsDataplane::load. Re-RUN the boot fixtures under Lima (`cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests`), NOT `--no-run` — this swap touches the composition root, so a compile-check is necessary-but-NOT-sufficient (project lesson: a --no-run gate is green even when every fixture refuses at boot).

DELETE the orphaned typed-error variants in the SAME commit (clippy -D warnings will flag them as dead): `MtlsBootError::Load { #[from] MtlsDataplaneError }` (error.rs:362-380) AND its `MtlsDataplaneError`-carrying siblings `InterceptError::OutboundAttach(#[from] MtlsDataplaneError)` (mtls_intercept_worker.rs:108) + `MtlsInterceptError::Dataplane { source: MtlsDataplaneError }` (mtls_intercept_worker.rs:763) — all die with the cgroup/MtlsDataplane surface. Drop/repurpose, never leave a `#[allow(dead_code)]` stub. (`MtlsBootError::Probe` STAYS — it wraps the UNCHANGED `MtlsEnforcement::probe`.)

WORKER new() PARAM-DROP + field/struct deletion (mtls_intercept_worker.rs): drop the `dataplane: MtlsDataplane` (:276) and `cgroup_root: PathBuf` (:277) params from `MtlsInterceptWorker::new` (:274); delete the `dataplane: Mutex<MtlsDataplane>` (:253) and `cgroup_root: PathBuf` (:256) struct fields; delete the `_cgroup_link: MtlsCgroupLink` field on `AllocIntercept` (:172) and the `attach_alloc` call in `start_alloc` (:354). Update EVERY `MtlsInterceptWorker::new(...)` call site (the only production one is lib.rs:1915; verify the worker test files do not construct it with the old shape — `mtls_intercept_fail_closed.rs` does at :104 and dies with this surface, `mtls_intercept_install.rs` does NOT construct it).

Grep the WHOLE workspace for `MtlsDataplane`, `cgroup_connect4_mtls`, `MTLS_REDIRECT_DEST`, `attach_alloc`, `program_redirect`, `unprogram_redirect`, `MtlsCgroupLink`, `MtlsDestKey`, `MtlsAddrPort`, `OutboundAttach`, `MtlsBootError::Load` and delete/rewire EVERY reference (src + tests + the BOTH `pub use` re-exports in overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/mod.rs:77) in this commit — no orphan may survive the swap (clippy -D warnings catches any miss). The two false positives to NOT touch: `cgroup_connect4_service` (the LB service program, a DIFFERENT program) in local_backend_proto_connect.rs and backend_discovery_bridge/walking_skeleton.rs are NOT this surface.

DELETE the superseded OLD-mechanism test files whole (deletion discipline: do NOT salvage-by-rewrite; the new mechanism's coverage is written FRESH in 03-03 + 05-01 + 05-03): `overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/mtls_dataplane_outbound_install.rs` (defends MtlsDataplane load+attach+program_redirect) and `mtls_composed_walking_skeleton.rs` (the OLD cgroup-mechanism composed test, superseded by the new 05-01 Tier-3 walking skeleton); `overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/mtls_outbound_enforce.rs` (asserts the OUTBOUND per-direction agent-light enforce-substrate asymmetry — forward `write_all` COPY / return `splice` — BUT its connection SETUP is the deleted cgroup-rewrite `OutboundWorkload`/`program_redirect_dest` mechanism; per CLAUDE.md deletion discipline a test coupled to a deleted production surface is DELETED, NOT salvaged-by-rewrite. Its enforce-substrate asymmetry is load-bearing coverage and is RE-ESTABLISHED FRESH on the Path-A egress nft-TPROXY mechanism in the new step 05-03 — NOT a rewrite of this file); `overdrive-worker/tests/integration/mtls_intercept_fail_closed.rs` (defends the OutboundAttach cgroup-attach failure path); `overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/mtls_production_activation.rs` + `mtls_e2e_helpers.rs` (the OLD cgroup + declared-peer activation e2e — references the whole deleted surface). Remove their `mod` declarations from the three `tests/integration.rs` entrypoints (overdrive-dataplane:52,59 + the `mtls_outbound_enforce` mod line; overdrive-worker:80; overdrive-control-plane).

PRUNE (not delete) the shared helper `overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/helpers/mtls_roles.rs`: the file is SHARED by SURVIVING tests (mtls_agent_handshake, mtls_inbound_enforce, mtls_guardrails consume `MtlsTopology`/`mtls_roles`), so DO NOT delete it. Remove ONLY the orphaned cgroup-outbound harness within it — `OutboundWorkload`, `OutboundPeer`, `spawn_outbound_workload`, `OutboundWorkload::run`, and `program_redirect_dest` (:528-606, which attach `cgroup_connect4_mtls` + program `MTLS_REDIRECT_DEST`). This prune is UNBLOCKED: the only consumer of the cgroup-outbound harness was `mtls_outbound_enforce.rs`, which is now DELETED whole (above) — so `OutboundWorkload`/`OutboundPeer`/`spawn_outbound_workload`/`program_redirect_dest` are orphaned and MUST be pruned (clippy -D warnings / dead_code makes leaving them fail the gate). Verify the surviving consumers (mtls_agent_handshake, mtls_inbound_enforce, mtls_guardrails) reference ONLY the inbound/shared helpers and leave those intact. `helpers/mtls_netns_topology.rs` is consumed by 4 surviving tests → do NOT touch it.
A test file shrinking is the correct shape of this deletion (CLAUDE.md § Deletion discipline: a deletion PR that adds tests on net is suspect).
steps.implementation_notes:
ADR-0071 fact 6 + Consequences/Net deletion. DELETIONS sequenced per dispatch: the step that swaps start_alloc to nft-TPROXY (orphaning the cgroup path) DELETES the cgroup-attach surface in the SAME commit. MtlsInterceptWorker::start_alloc at mtls_intercept_worker.rs:334; the cgroup install + MTLS_REDIRECT_DEST narrative is in the module rustdoc :41-42 and start_alloc :406-415. bpf.md: cgroup_connect4_mtls is the last outbound kernel-side program; Path A adds NO kernel-side program. CLAUDE.md § Deletion discipline + feedback memory 'Delete unused code AND its tests': dead_code warning = MORE to delete, not a gate to add. development.md § 'src/ is production code': delete, don't gate. Grep ALL tests across crates before committing. Test tier: Tier-3 (both rules install on a real alloc) + compile/clippy gate.

MtlsDataplane::load SERVICE_MAP FATE — RESOLVED BY CODE INVESTIGATION (delete the whole MtlsDataplane struct). MtlsDataplane::load (overdrive-dataplane/src/mtls/dataplane.rs:189) does TWO things: (1) recover cgroup_connect4_mtls + MTLS_REDIRECT_DEST (Path A orphans this), AND (2) a reuse-or-first-pin of the phase-2 SERVICE_MAP HoM (dataplane.rs:204-235). The SERVICE_MAP work is REDUNDANT in production: EbpfDataplane::new_with_pin_dir INDEPENDENTLY pre-creates + pre-pins SERVICE_MAP via HashOfMapsHandle::new_pinned_with_array_inner (overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs:446-461), and in run_server that runs at lib.rs:1519-1544 — BEFORE MtlsDataplane::load runs at lib.rs:1828. dataplane.rs's own comment at :196-203 states this verbatim: 'In production EbpfDataplane (run_server) pins SERVICE_MAP before MtlsDataplane::load runs, so the BPF_OBJ_GET branch is taken and this surface NEVER touches the live pin... The create branch fires ONLY when no prior owner pinned it (the standalone Tier-3 test).' Verified: EbpfDataplane is the independent+earlier pinner; the MtlsDataplane SERVICE_MAP block is a first-pinner fallback for the standalone Tier-3 test path ONLY, which dies with mtls_dataplane_outbound_install.rs. CONCLUSION: SERVICE_MAP pinning is NOT owned by MtlsDataplane::load — DELETE the WHOLE MtlsDataplane struct + load() + the redirect_dest map + MtlsDestKey/MtlsAddrPort PODs + attach_alloc/program_redirect/unprogram_redirect/MtlsCgroupLink (dataplane.rs entire file) + the `pub use dataplane::{MtlsCgroupLink, MtlsDataplane, MtlsDataplaneError}` re-export (mtls/mod.rs:77) + `pub mod dataplane;` (mtls/mod.rs:68). NO SERVICE_MAP relocation is needed (EbpfDataplane already owns it). The pin_dir arg threaded into the MtlsDataplane::load call (lib.rs:1820-1823) dies with the load block.

steps.name: Per-connection resolve consumer in the outbound accept loop — DELETE the declared-peer surface in the SAME commit
criteria: EXTEND the outbound accept loop: after `getsockname` recovers orig_dst, call the injected `MtlsResolve` port. `Mesh(b)` -> set `Routed::Outbound { peer: b.addr }` (expected_peer stays None until #178) and call `enforce`; `NonMesh` -> pass-through (cleartext, by design); `MeshUnreachable` -> fail-closed (refuse, NO cleartext). The per-arm decision is driven by the variant, never inferred from a sentinel (C1).

DELETION (single-cut, SAME commit): DELETE `program_declared_peer_redirect` (the test-only #178 stand-in, mtls_intercept_worker.rs:547) + its `MtlsInterceptError` return type (the `UnknownAlloc`/`Dataplane` enum at :753 — note `MtlsInterceptError::Dataplane`'s `MtlsDataplaneError` source already dies in 04-01), the `real_peer` and `leg_f_addr` declared-peer slots on `AllocIntercept` (:202/:233) and on the `AcceptLeg::Outbound` arm (:616/:778), the `AcceptOutcome::Dropped` no-declared-peer arm (:645/:789) and `accept_drop_outbound` (:850) — AND every test defending them. The declared-peer TESTS live in `mtls_intercept_worker.rs`'s OWN inline `#[cfg(test)]` module (already in files_to_modify) — there is NO separate worker integration test file defending this surface (verified: `overdrive-worker/tests/integration/mtls_intercept_install.rs` does NOT reference `program_declared_peer_redirect`/`real_peer` — it tests the SURVIVING inbound nft-TPROXY + leg-acquire free functions; do NOT delete it). NO #[allow(dead_code)], NO stubs.

FIX the broken rustdoc intra-doc link: `overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` carries an intra-doc reference to `MtlsInterceptWorker::program_declared_peer_redirect` (lib.rs:981-986, on the `#[cfg(integration-tests)] mtls_worker(...)` accessor) — it goes dead when the method is deleted (clippy/rustdoc broken-intra-doc-link fails the gate). Fix the doc link in this commit (re-point to the surviving accessor purpose, or drop the dangling `[...]` reference). The `mtls_production_activation.rs`/`mtls_e2e_helpers.rs` that ALSO drove `program_declared_peer_redirect` are DELETED whole in 04-01 (they reference the cgroup surface too), so they are not re-listed here.
The step that adds the resolve consumer (orphaning the declared-peer slots) IS the step that deletes them (dispatch sequencing rule).

`MtlsResolve` is injected as a mandatory constructor parameter on the worker (Arc<dyn MtlsResolve>), NOT defaulted/builder-optional (development.md § Port-trait dependencies: required-not-defaulted, no builder override) — tests pass SimMtlsResolve, production passes ServiceBackendsResolve.

Default-lane DST: drive the accept loop against SimMtlsResolve scripted to each arm; assert Mesh->enforce-called-with-peer==b.addr, NonMesh->cleartext-pass-through, MeshUnreachable->refused-no-cleartext. Authn-only boundary: the test MUST NOT call the wrong-but-valid-peer case 'protected' (expected_peer is None until #178).
Mutation >= 80% on the 3-arm decision logic (the arm-match mutations are the canonical bug shape — a dropped MeshUnreachable arm = silent cleartext).
Compile-check + clippy -D warnings clean after the deletion (Lima-routed).
steps.implementation_notes: ADR-0071 fact 4 (enrollment per-connection resolve replaces the per-destination map) + C1 (the 3-arm decision IS the variant). DELETIONS sequenced per dispatch: the step that adds the resolve consumer (orphaning the declared-peer slots) DELETES the declared-peer surface in the SAME commit. Declared-peer surface: program_declared_peer_redirect (mtls_intercept_worker.rs:547), real_peer Arc<Mutex<Option<SocketAddrV4>>> (:233), leg_f_addr/real_peer on AllocIntercept + AcceptLeg::Outbound, AcceptOutcome::Dropped (:789) + accept_drop_outbound (:850). development.md § Port-trait dependencies: MtlsResolve is a MANDATORY new() param (no builder, no default — tests forget builders). Q4/D-TME-8 authn-only: expected_peer None until #178; do NOT thread IdentityRead, do NOT call wrong-valid-peer 'protected'. CLAUDE.md § Deletion discipline. Test tier: default-lane DST + mutation; the composed real-kernel proof is 05-01. CROSS-STEP NOTE (dep on 04-01): 04-01 already deletes the cgroup/MtlsDataplane surface AND the production_activation/e2e_helpers test files that drove BOTH the cgroup path and program_declared_peer_redirect; `MtlsInterceptError::Dataplane`'s MtlsDataplaneError source also dies in 04-01. So 04-02's remaining declared-peer deletion is the in-worker-src surface (program_declared_peer_redirect, real_peer/leg_f_addr slots, AcceptOutcome::Dropped, accept_drop_outbound, the MtlsInterceptError enum) + its inline tests + the lib.rs:981-986 broken doc link. VERIFIED CORRECTION: the dispatch's reference to `overdrive-worker/tests/integration/mtls_intercept_install.rs` defending program_declared_peer_redirect/real_peer is WRONG — that file does NOT reference the declared-peer surface (it tests the surviving inbound nft-TPROXY + leg-acquire free functions); it is NOT a deletion target. COMPOSITION-ROOT ORDERING REQUIREMENT (from 01-03 C4/D-TME-11, REVISED 2026-06-17 by the resolve-index-coherence research): the production ServiceBackendsResolve adapter (mtls_resolve_adapter.rs) builds its in-RAM addr→Backend index via List-then-Watch + relist-on-`Lagged` — List-at-probe via the keyless all_service_backends_rows enumerate, Watch via subscribe_all, re-List on `broadcast::RecvError::Lagged`. The List-at-probe leg SUPERSEDES the prior strict subscribe-before-first-write requirement: the boot-time List captures any pre-existing service_backends rows (gossiped or persisted, e.g. on a control-plane restart), so the adapter is robust to rows written BEFORE it starts — the index is never empty-but-trusted during convergence. KEEP the 'probe at boot before serving' ordering: when wiring ServiceBackendsResolve into the worker in lib.rs run_server, the adapter MUST be constructed AND its probe() run at boot before any connection is resolved (the Earned-Trust gate still gates boot, wire→probe→use). The List-at-probe captures pre-existing rows; the probe still gates boot. #237 (the cold-start restart-window instance) is CLOSED by the 01-03 List-at-probe + relist mechanism (the GH issue closes once the crafter lands it). A miss remains NonMesh by design (never fail-closed); the residual irreducible convergence window is covered by (a) fail-toward-handshake — the v1 SECURITY invariant 'a resolve miss must never silently emit cleartext to a should-be-mesh peer' — whose code lands under #236. The probe is the Earned-Trust gate: an adapter that cannot read service_backends at boot refuses with health.startup.refused (principle 12, wire→probe→use).

steps.name: Action-shim C3 wiring: provision the per-workload netns at on_alloc_running, BEFORE Driver::start AND start_alloc; teardown at on_alloc_terminal
criteria: C3 ENFORCED: the per-workload netns+veth provisioner (02-*) is invoked from the action-shim StartAllocation arm BEFORE `driver.start(&spec)` (the netns must exist before the ExecDriver `setns(CLONE_NEWNET)` seam enters it — driver.rs:198) AND therefore before `worker.start_alloc`. Teardown of the netns is wired at `on_alloc_terminal` (alongside the existing `worker.stop_alloc` / `driver.on_alloc_terminal`).

The driver's `netns_path: Option<PathBuf>` (driver.rs:198) is wired to the per-alloc netns the provisioner created, so `Driver::start` enters it via the existing pre_exec setns hook (reuse the seam as-is; CNI-aligned — driver ENTERS, never creates).

Fail-closed ordering preserved: if netns provisioning fails, the alloc MUST NOT run (the provisioner refuses; the StartAllocation arm surfaces a Failed row, mirroring the existing fail_closed_on_mtls_install precedent at action_shim/mod.rs:982) — no cleartext, no never-converged netns.

Composition-root invariant (wire-then-probe-then-use, principle 12): the resolve adapter probe + the provisioner converge both gate boot/alloc-start; an adapter/provisioner that fails its probe refuses with health.startup.refused.

Tier-3 acceptance: a real exec alloc reaching Running has its netns+veth provisioned (with resolv.conf injected) before the workload process is spawned into the netns; on terminal the netns is torn down with zero residue.

Default-lane DST around the shim ordering where injectable (SimDriver + a sim provisioner seam): provision-before-start ordering is asserted; provision-failure -> Failed-row, never Running-with-no-netns.
steps.implementation_notes: C3 PINNED (ADR-0071 fact 1 + § 'Driving ports' + DESIGN-review suggestion 3). The action-shim StartAllocation arm (action_shim/mod.rs:866) currently calls driver.start (:887) BEFORE worker.start_alloc (:980) BEFORE driver.on_alloc_running (:1002). The NEW netns-provision call must be inserted BEFORE driver.start (:887) — this is a design-coherent insertion point (the netns must pre-exist the setns seam), NOT an invented surface; the provisioner from 02-* is the callee. Teardown wires alongside the existing on_alloc_terminal arms (:851/:857, :1227/:1232). FLAGGED GAP (see report): C3 says 'on_alloc_running hook' but the current shim provisions/starts within the StartAllocation arm and only FIRES driver.on_alloc_running AFTER start — the netns provision is therefore an in-arm pre-driver.start step, not literally hung off the on_alloc_running callback. If the crafter finds the intended hook is genuinely the post-start on_alloc_running callback (which would be too late for setns), STOP and surface — do not relocate the seam. Fail-closed mirrors fail_closed_on_mtls_install (:982). Test tier: Tier-3 + default-lane DST on ordering.

phases.name: Composed bidirectional Tier-3 walking skeleton + name->resolve->enforce consistency (DNS stubbed until #61)

steps.name: Composed bidirectional walking skeleton (Tier-3): getsockname->resolve->enforce mTLS both directions, 0x17 wire capture, no RST
criteria: Tier-3 real-kernel walking skeleton (Lima, gated integration-tests) in the real netns/veth topology on the Path-A mechanism (ADR-0071 § Enforcement Tier-3 obligation (c)): a workload in its netns connects to a running mesh backend; the connection is captured (egress nft-TPROXY -> leg-F -> getsockname), resolved (Mesh), and enforced (mTLS to the backend); the server side is captured inbound (nft-TPROXY -> leg-C -> getsockname -> server SVID handshake). BOTH directions complete a round-trip.
Correctness oracle 1 (orig-dst): getsockname-recovered orig_dst == dialed dst on both legs.

Correctness oracle 2 (encryption on the wire): a tcpdump/wire capture on the veth shows a TLS 1.3 application-data record (0x17 record type) — proving the bytes are encrypted, not cleartext, end to end.

No RST post-arm: the connection is NOT reset after the intercept arms; normal AND traced (slow) timing both complete without RST/corruption (ADR-0069 Slice 00 obligation, now on the Path-A mechanism).

Enrollment correctness (obligation (d)): a captured connection resolving to a running mesh backend drives mTLS to that backend; a connection resolving to no mesh peer is classified (NonMesh pass-through / MeshUnreachable fail-closed per Q3); NEVER silent cleartext to a should-be-mesh peer. Exercise all three arms end-to-end.
F5 holds in the composed flow: the agent's leg-B/leg-S dials are not re-captured; a workload cannot self-exempt (obligation (b), composed).

Test at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/bidirectional_walking_skeleton.rs (or overdrive-control-plane if full boot composition is needed); run via cargo xtask lima run --; NO --no-run; records uname -r.
Authn-only boundary respected: the test asserts encryption + correct-backend authn, and MUST NOT assert intended-peer 'protection' (expected_peer None until #178).
steps.implementation_notes: THE WALKING SKELETON (ADR-0071 § Enforcement Tier-3 obligations (b)+(c)+(d)). Composes everything: 02 (netns+veth+resolv.conf), 03 (egress capture+getsockname), 04 (start_alloc both rules + resolve consumer + C3 netns wiring). Correctness oracle per dispatch: getsockname orig-dst == dialed dst AND a 0x17 TLS 1.3 application-data record on the wire. ADR-0069 enforcement substrate (kTLS/rustls/agent-light splice) is UNCHANGED — reuse it. Q4 authn-only: encryption + chain-to-bundle authn only; NOT intended-peer pinning (that is #178). Tier-3 assertion rules (testing.md): observable kernel/wire side effects (tcpdump 0x17, getsockname, no RST), never program-internal reachability. Test tier: Tier-3 real-kernel under Lima.

steps.name: name->resolve->enforce consistency (Tier-3, DNS stubbed until #61): DNS-returned service_backends addr IS the addr MtlsResolve recognizes (single-source invariant)
criteria: Tier-3 acceptance (ADR-0071 § Enforcement Tier-3 obligation (e), Q5a): the single-source invariant — a running service_backends addr B is what DNS would return (headless, D-TME-10) AND `MtlsResolve.resolve(getsockname-recovered B)` returns `Mesh(ResolvedBackend { addr: B, .. })`. The DNS-returned addr IS the addr the resolve port recognizes, because both read the same service_backends source (one source, two readers, byte-consistent).

DNS STUBBED (the #61 responder daemon is NOT built here): the workload connects to a KNOWN service_backends addr directly (the responder step is stubbed), so the resolve-recognizes-orig_dst half is validated independently of the responder. ADR-0071 § Enforcement obligation (e): 'Until #61's responder lands, this is exercised with the DNS step stubbed.'

The resolv.conf injection (02-03) is asserted present in the netns (the injection mechanism this feature OWNS), even though the responder it points at is the #61 stub — proving the injection wires correctly without building the daemon.

Optionally, if a minimal test-only DNS stub responder is stood up (reading the same service_backends test dataset), the full getaddrinfo->connect(B)->capture->resolve(B)->enforce flow is exercised end-to-end with the stub standing in for #61 — but the daemon itself is NOT productionised (scope guardrail).
SCOPE GUARDRAIL re-asserted: this step builds NO #61 daemon, NO #167 VIP allocator. Headless v1 only.
Test at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/name_resolve_enforce_consistency.rs; run via cargo xtask lima run --; NO --no-run.

Default-lane DST companion: SimMtlsResolve scripted with addr B + the same B fed as orig_dst asserts the single-source recognition without a real kernel (the cheap reproduction; Tier-3 is the trust signal).
steps.implementation_notes: ADR-0071 fact 10 + § Enforcement obligation (e) + Q5a/D-TME-9/D-TME-10. The single-source invariant: DNS returns a service_backends addr; MtlsResolve reads a service_backends addr; no translation. HEADLESS v1 (D-TME-10) — DNS returns a running backend addr, NOT a VIP. SCOPE GUARDRAIL (dispatch): the #61 DNS responder daemon and the #167 VIP allocator are named dependencies, NOT built here — DNS is STUBBED (workload connects to a known service_backends addr directly). Building a productionised responder = scope creep. The injection MECHANISM (resolv.conf) IS this feature's (02-03); the responder it points at is #61. Test tier: Tier-3 (real netns, stubbed DNS) + default-lane DST companion (cheap reproduction).

steps.name: Outbound enforce-substrate per-direction agent-light asymmetry (Tier-3) — re-establish FRESH on the Path-A egress nft-TPROXY mechanism
criteria: Tier-3 real-kernel acceptance (Lima, gated `integration-tests`): on a Path-A egress-captured OUTBOUND connection (workload in its netns -> egress nft-TPROXY -> leg-F -> getsockname -> resolve(Mesh) -> enforce), assert the agent-light enforce substrate's per-direction asymmetry is UNCHANGED from ADR-0069: the forward direction (plaintext workload -> ciphertext backend) uses a `write_all` COPY, and the return direction (ciphertext backend -> plaintext workload) uses `splice`. The asymmetry is the ADR-0069 substrate carried forward VERBATIM by Path A — this step proves it survives the mechanism swap, on the new egress path.

FRESH against the new mechanism: the connection SETUP is the Path-A egress nft-TPROXY harness (real netns/veth + egress capture), NOT the deleted cgroup-rewrite `OutboundWorkload`/`program_redirect_dest`. This is a NEW test, NOT a rewrite of the deleted `mtls_outbound_enforce.rs` — do NOT reference `OutboundWorkload`, `OutboundPeer`, `spawn_outbound_workload`, or `program_redirect_dest`. Symmetric in structure with the SURVIVING `mtls_inbound_enforce.rs`.

Assertions on observable syscall/wire side effects only (the substrate's directional copy strategy is observable via the enforce path's behaviour / wire capture / syscall trace), never on program-internal reachability (testing.md Tier-3 assertion rules).
Authn-only boundary (Q4) respected: assert encryption + the substrate asymmetry, NOT intended-peer 'protection' (expected_peer is None until #178).

Test at crates/overdrive-worker/tests/integration/outbound_enforce_substrate_asymmetry.rs, wired through tests/integration.rs; run via `cargo xtask lima run --`; NO `--no-run`; records uname -r (spike.md: verdict pinned to a kernel).

ADR-0069 enforcement substrate (kTLS/rustls/agent-light splice) is UNCHANGED — reuse it; this step does NOT modify the substrate, only re-establishes its directional-asymmetry coverage on the Path-A capture path.
steps.implementation_notes: RE-ESTABLISHES FRESH the enforce-substrate coverage that the DELETED `overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/mtls_outbound_enforce.rs` (deleted whole in 04-01) provided. WHY FRESH, NOT SALVAGED: the old test's connection SETUP was the deleted cgroup-rewrite `OutboundWorkload`/`program_redirect_dest` mechanism; per CLAUDE.md § Deletion discipline a test coupled to a deleted production surface is deleted, not salvaged-by-rewrite. The ASSERTION it carried — the OUTBOUND per-direction agent-light asymmetry (forward `write_all` COPY / return `splice`) — is load-bearing ADR-0069 substrate coverage UNCHANGED by Path A (ADR-0069 carries forward verbatim), and MUST NOT be lost. So this step re-writes that assertion FRESH against the new Path-A egress nft-TPROXY setup, symmetric with the surviving `mtls_inbound_enforce.rs`. Deps: 04-02 (the resolve consumer driving enforce on the outbound accept loop) + 03-03 (the real egress capture / getsockname recovery the setup builds on). Q4 authn-only: encryption + substrate asymmetry only; NOT intended-peer pinning (#178). Test tier: Tier-3 real-kernel under Lima.
